model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Michigan is the safer Sweet 16 pick because its full-season profile is dramatically stronger overall, with top-two rankings in both KenPom and Torvik and an elite defense that is far sturdier than Alabama’s. Alabama absolutely has a credible upset path because its No. 3-caliber offense can win any one-game shootout, but the Tide’s much weaker defensive baseline gives Michigan the better two-way floor in this matchup. With no clearly confirmed availability issue large enough to swing the game, I’d advance the Wolverines.

reasoningStep.title: Michigan’s balance over Alabama’s volatility
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan owns the much stronger overall efficiency profile and defensive edge, while Alabama’s elite offense keeps the upset path real but not preferable.
evidence: Michigan is No. 2 in both KenPom (AdjEM 38.38) and Torvik, with adjusted offense around No. 7-8 and adjusted defense around No. 2.
Alabama is No. 16 in KenPom and No. 17 in Torvik, with an elite No. 3 offense but a much weaker defense ranked around No. 67 by Torvik.
Records also favor Michigan substantially, 31-2 versus Alabama’s 23-9, reinforcing the larger full-season quality gap.
Recent news searches did not surface a clearly confirmed injury or availability absence significant enough to materially change the matchup outlook.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
